---
title: "Copula"
author: "Guanyu"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Raw data

```{r cars}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)

simd <- readr::read_csv("simd2020_withgeog/simd2020_withinds.csv")
simd_shp <- sf::st_read("simd2020_withgeog/sc_dz_11.shp")
simd_joined <- simd_shp %>%
  left_join(simd, by = c("DataZone" = "Data_Zone"))
joint_board_lookup <- tribble(
  ~Council_area,                          ~Joint_Board_area,
  "East Ayrshire",                       "Ayrshire",
  "North Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "South Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "Scottish Borders",                    "Borders",
  "Clackmannanshire",                    "Central Scotland",
  "Falkirk",                             "Central Scotland",
  "Stirling",                            "Central Scotland",
  "Dumfries and Galloway",               "Dumfries and Galloway",
  "Argyll and Bute",                     "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "East Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "West Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "Fife",                                "Fife",
  "Aberdeen City",                       "Grampian",
  "Aberdeenshire",                       "Grampian",
  "Moray",                               "Grampian",
  "Glasgow City",                        "Glasgow",
  "Highland",                            "Highlands and Islands",
  "Na h-Eileanan an Iar",                "Highlands and Islands",
  "North Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "South Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "East Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "City of Edinburgh",                   "Lothian",
  "Midlothian",                          "Lothian",
  "West Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "Orkney Islands",                      "Orkney and Shetland",
  "Shetland Islands",                    "Orkney and Shetland",
  "East Renfrewshire",                   "Renfrewshire",
  "Inverclyde",                          "Renfrewshire",
  "Renfrewshire",                        "Renfrewshire",
  "Angus",                               "Tayside",
  "Dundee City",                         "Tayside",
  "Perth and Kinross",                   "Tayside"
)

simd_joined <- simd_joined %>%
  left_join(joint_board_lookup, by = c("Council_area" = "Council_area"))

# Pull out Lothian data

indicator_cols <- c(
"income_rate", "income_count",
"employment_rate", "employment_count",
"CIF", "ALCOHOL", "DRUG", "SMR", "DEPRESS",
"LBWT", "EMERG", "Attendance", "Attainment",
"no_qualifications", "not_participating", "University",
"crime_count", "crime_rate",
"overcrowded_count", "nocentralheating_count",
"overcrowded_rate", "nocentralheating_rate",
"drive_petrol", "drive_GP", "drive_post",
"drive_primary", "drive_retail", "drive_secondary",
"PT_GP", "PT_post", "PT_retail", "broadband"
)

simd_lothian_raw <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(DataZone, any_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.character) & !matches("^DataZone$"), 
      ~ as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))
      )
    )

na_summary <- simd_lothian_raw %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      all_of(indicator_cols),
      list(
        Missing_Count   = ~ sum(is.na(.)),
        Percent_Missing = ~ mean(is.na(.)) * 100
        ),
      .names = "{.col}.{.fn}")
    ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to  = c("Indicator", ".value"),
    names_sep = "\\.") %>%
  arrange(desc(Missing_Count))


lothian_ind <- na_summary %>%
  filter(Percent_Missing <= 10) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

simd_lothian <- simd_lothian_raw %>%
  select(DataZone, all_of(lothian_ind)) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(lothian_ind))

# Standardize for PCA & clustering

lothian_data <- simd_lothian %>%
  select(-DataZone) %>%
  scale() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Domain Mapping

indicator_domains <- tribble(
  ~Indicator,                ~Domain,
  # Income
  "income_rate",             "Income",
  "income_count",            "Income",

  # Employment
  "employment_rate",         "Employment",
  "employment_count",        "Employment",

  # Health
  "CIF",                     "Health",
  "ALCOHOL",                 "Health",
  "DRUG",                    "Health",
  "SMR",                     "Health",
  "DEPRESS",                 "Health",
  "LBWT",                    "Health",
  "EMERG",                   "Health",

  # Education
  "Attendance",              "Education",
  "Attainment",              "Education",
  "no_qualifications",       "Education",
  "not_participating",       "Education",
  "University",              "Education",

  # Access
  "drive_petrol",            "Access",
  "drive_GP",                "Access",
  "drive_post",              "Access",
  "drive_primary",           "Access",
  "drive_retail",            "Access",
  "drive_secondary",         "Access",
  "PT_GP",                   "Access",
  "PT_post",                 "Access",
  "PT_retail",               "Access",
  "broadband",               "Access",

  # Crime
  "crime_count",             "Crime",
  "crime_rate",              "Crime",

  # Housing
  "overcrowded_count",       "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_count",  "Housing",
  "overcrowded_rate",        "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_rate",   "Housing"
)

domain_vars <- indicator_domains %>%
  filter(Indicator %in% colnames(lothian_data)) %>%
  group_by(Domain) %>%
  summarise(vars = list(Indicator)) %>%
  deframe()


raw_ind <- indicator_domains %>%
  filter(Domain %in% c("Health", "Education", "Access")) %>%
  filter(!Indicator %in% c("Attendance")) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

rate_ind <- c("income_rate", "employment_rate", "crime_rate",
         "nocentralheating_rate", "overcrowded_rate")

raw_data <- lothian_data %>% select(all_of(c(raw_ind,rate_ind)))
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
k <- 2
k_raw <- kmeans(raw_data, centers = k, nstart = 25)
lothian_data <- raw_data %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_k = k_raw$cluster
  )
```

## R-vine
```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=6, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(rvinecopulib)
library(ggraph)

# independence test 

X <- lothian_data %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  select(-any_of("cluster_k")) %>%
  as.data.frame() # Keeps only the variables

X <- X %>% drop_na()

U <- pseudo_obs(as.matrix(X))

fit <- vinecop(
  data = U,
  family_set = "parametric",  # search common parametric families
  selcrit = "bic",            # penalizes complexity more than AIC
  tree_crit = "tau",          # dependence measure for structure selection
  trunc_lvl = Inf,            # fit all trees
  cores = 4                   # parallelize if possible
)

fit
summary(fit)
plot(fit)

logLik(fit)
AIC(fit)
BIC(fit)

```

```{r}
fit_cluster_vines <- function(df, cluster_col = "cluster_k", cores = 4) {
  clusters <- sort(unique(df[[cluster_col]]))

  res <- lapply(clusters, function(k) {
    Xk <- df %>%
      filter(.data[[cluster_col]] == k) %>%
      select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
      select(-any_of(cluster_col)) %>%
      drop_na() %>%
      as.data.frame()

    Uk <- pseudo_obs(as.matrix(Xk))

    fitk <- vinecop(
      data = Uk,
      family_set = "parametric",
      selcrit = "bic",
      tree_crit = "tau",
      trunc_lvl = Inf,
      cores = cores
    )

    list(k = k, X = Xk, U = Uk, fit = fitk)
  })

  names(res) <- paste0("cluster_", clusters)
  res
}

fits <- fit_cluster_vines(lothian_data, cores = 4)

edge_table <- function(fit) {
  sm <- summary(fit)  # data-frame-like

  vn <- attr(sm, "var_names")
  if (is.null(vn) || length(vn) == 0) vn <- paste0("V", seq_len(ncol(fit$data)))

  sm$var1 <- vn[sapply(sm$conditioned, `[[`, 1)]
  sm$var2 <- vn[sapply(sm$conditioned, `[[`, 2)]

  sm$cond_set <- sapply(sm$conditioning, function(idx) {
    idx <- idx[idx != 0]
    if (length(idx) == 0) "" else paste(vn[idx], collapse = ", ")
  })

  sm
}

edges_by_cluster <- lapply(fits, function(obj) edge_table(obj$fit))

edges_all <- bind_rows(lapply(names(edges_by_cluster), function(nm) {
  df <- edges_by_cluster[[nm]]
  df %>%
    mutate(
      cluster = nm,
      pair = paste(var1, var2, sep = " — "),
      abs_tau = abs(tau)
    )
}))

top_tree1 <- edges_all %>% filter(tree == 1) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>% slice_max(abs_tau, n = 12, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(top_tree1, aes(x = abs_tau, y = reorder(pair, abs_tau))) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "|Kendall's tau| (Tree 1)", y = NULL)
```

```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=6, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(patchwork)

fit1 <- fits[["cluster_1"]]$fit
fit2 <- fits[["cluster_2"]]$fit

p1 <- plot(fit1, tree = 1, edge_labels = "tau") +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Cluster 1 — Tree 1")

p2 <- plot(fit2, tree = 1, var_names = "legend", edge_labels = "tau") +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Cluster 2 — Tree 1")

p1 | p2

```

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height= 7.8, dpi=300}
# ============================================================
# Chunk: rvinecopulib cluster-wise RVine + CVine + readable plots
# (replaces the old VineCopula::RVineStructureSelect workflow)
# ============================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(rvinecopulib)
  library(patchwork)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggraph)   # required by plot.vinecop()
})

# ----------------------------
# 0) Helpers
# ----------------------------

make_U <- function(X) {
  U <- rvinecopulib::pseudo_obs(as.matrix(X))
  colnames(U) <- colnames(X)
  U
}

get_cluster_X <- function(df, k, cluster_col = "cluster_k") {
  df %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data[[cluster_col]] == k) %>%
    dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
    dplyr::select(-any_of(cluster_col)) %>%
    tidyr::drop_na() %>%
    as.data.frame()
}

# Choose a C-vine order by "overall dependence":
# same spirit as common C-vine heuristics (sum of |Kendall tau| per variable).
cvine_order_from_tau <- function(U) {
  tau_mat <- stats::cor(U, method = "kendall", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  score <- colSums(abs(tau_mat), na.rm = TRUE)
  order(score, decreasing = TRUE)
}

fit_one_cluster_rvinecopulib <- function(df, k,
                                         cluster_col = "cluster_k",
                                         type = c("RVine", "CVine"),
                                         family_set = "parametric",
                                         selcrit = "bic",
                                         tree_crit = "tau",
                                         trunc_lvl = Inf,
                                         threshold = 0,
                                         cores = 4,
                                         cvine_order = NULL) {
  type <- match.arg(type)

  Xk <- get_cluster_X(df, k, cluster_col = cluster_col)
  Uk <- make_U(Xk)

  if (type == "RVine") {
    vc <- rvinecopulib::vinecop(
      data = Uk,
      family_set = family_set,
      structure = NA,      # automatic (regular) vine structure selection
      selcrit = selcrit,
      tree_crit = tree_crit,
      trunc_lvl = trunc_lvl,
      threshold = threshold,
      cores = cores
    )
    list(cluster = k, X = Xk, U = Uk, fit = vc, type = "RVine")
  } else {
    if (is.null(cvine_order)) cvine_order <- cvine_order_from_tau(Uk)
    cv_struct <- rvinecopulib::cvine_structure(cvine_order)

    vc <- rvinecopulib::vinecop(
      data = Uk,
      family_set = family_set,
      structure = cv_struct,  # fixed C-vine structure
      selcrit = selcrit,
      tree_crit = tree_crit,
      trunc_lvl = trunc_lvl,
      threshold = threshold,
      cores = cores
    )
    list(cluster = k, X = Xk, U = Uk, fit = vc, type = "CVine",
         cvine_order = cvine_order, structure = cv_struct)
  }
}

fit_clusterwise_vines <- function(df,
                                  cluster_col = "cluster_k",
                                  clusters = NULL,
                                  type = c("RVine", "CVine"),
                                  family_set = "parametric",
                                  selcrit = "bic",
                                  tree_crit = "tau",
                                  trunc_lvl = Inf,
                                  threshold = 0,
                                  cores = 4) {
  type <- match.arg(type)
  if (is.null(clusters)) clusters <- sort(unique(df[[cluster_col]]))

  fits <- purrr::map(clusters, ~ fit_one_cluster_rvinecopulib(
    df = df, k = .x, cluster_col = cluster_col,
    type = type,
    family_set = family_set,
    selcrit = selcrit,
    tree_crit = tree_crit,
    trunc_lvl = trunc_lvl,
    threshold = threshold,
    cores = cores
  ))

  names(fits) <- paste0("cluster_", clusters)
  fits
}

# ----------------------------
# 1) Fit RVine and CVine (rvinecopulib)
# ----------------------------

fits_rvine <- fit_clusterwise_vines(
  df = lothian_data,
  cluster_col = "cluster_k",
  type = "RVine",
  family_set = "parametric",
  selcrit = "bic",
  tree_crit = "tau",
  trunc_lvl = Inf,
  threshold = 0,
  cores = 4
)

fits_cvine <- fit_clusterwise_vines(
  df = lothian_data,
  cluster_col = "cluster_k",
  type = "CVine",
  family_set = "parametric",
  selcrit = "bic",
  tree_crit = "tau",
  trunc_lvl = Inf,
  threshold = 0,
  cores = 4
)

# ----------------------------
# 2) Readable side-by-side plots
# ----------------------------
# Notes:
# - plot.vinecop() returns a ggraph/ggplot object, so patchwork works.
# - edge_labels options: "family", "tau", "family_tau", "pair"
# - var_names = "legend" uses node numbers in the graph and prints a legend mapping
#   numbers -> variables (this replaces the old custom NO-key you built).

plot_tree_side_by_side <- function(fits, tree = 1, main_prefix = "",
                                   edge_label = c("tau", "family_tau", "family", "pair", "auto", "tau+cond_no")) {
  edge_label <- match.arg(edge_label)
  # "tau+cond_no" was a custom label in your old VineCopula plotting code.
  # plot.vinecop() does not support it directly, so we fall back to "tau".
  if (edge_label == "tau+cond_no") edge_label <- "tau"
  if (edge_label == "auto") edge_label <- if (tree == 1) "tau" else "family_tau"

  fit1 <- fits[["cluster_1"]]$fit
  fit2 <- fits[["cluster_2"]]$fit

  p1 <- plot(fit1, tree = tree, var_names = "legend", edge_labels = edge_label) +
    ggtitle(paste0(main_prefix, "Cluster 1 — Tree ", tree))

  p2 <- plot(fit2, tree = tree, var_names = "legend", edge_labels = edge_label) +
    ggtitle(paste0(main_prefix, "Cluster 2 — Tree ", tree))

  (p1 | p2) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

# ======================
# Example usage:
# ======================
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_rvine, tree = 1, main_prefix = "RVine | ", edge_label = "tau")
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_rvine, tree = 2, main_prefix = "RVine | ", edge_label = "family_tau")
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_cvine, tree = 1, main_prefix = "CVine | ", edge_label = "tau")
plot_tree_side_by_side(fits_cvine, tree = 2, main_prefix = "CVine | ", edge_label = "family_tau")
```

```{r}
# =========================================
# Accurate bar charts: Cluster 1, Cluster 2, and Difference
# (robust to cluster naming and A-B vs B-A duplicates)
# =========================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

# Canonicalise pair key so A—B and B—A are identical
canon_pair <- function(a, b, sep = " — ") {
  ifelse(a <= b, paste(a, b, sep = sep), paste(b, a, sep = sep))
}

# Normalise cluster labels to C1/C2 (robust)
norm_cluster <- function(x) {
  x0 <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
  dplyr::case_when(
    x0 %in% c("cluster_1", "cluster 1", "c1", "1") ~ "C1",
    x0 %in% c("cluster_2", "cluster 2", "c2", "2") ~ "C2",
    TRUE ~ x  # fallback (shouldn't happen)
  )
}

# Build clean edge table for a given tree

extract_tree_edges <- function(vine, tree = 1, var_names = NULL) {
  if (is.null(vine)) stop("`vine` is NULL. Did you pass obj$fit?")
  sm <- summary(vine)

  if (is.null(var_names)) {
    if (!is.null(vine$names)) var_names <- vine$names
    else var_names <- as.character(seq_len(dim(vine)[1]))
  }

  idx_to_name <- function(idx) {
    idx <- as.integer(idx)
    if (length(idx) == 0 || is.na(idx)) return(NA_character_)
    if (idx >= 1 && idx <= length(var_names)) return(var_names[idx])
    as.character(idx)
  }

  sm %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data$tree == tree) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      var1 = vapply(.data$conditioned, function(z) idx_to_name(z[1]), character(1)),
      var2 = vapply(.data$conditioned, function(z) idx_to_name(z[2]), character(1)),
      cond_set = vapply(.data$conditioning, function(z) {
        z <- z[!is.na(z) & z != 0]
        if (length(z) == 0) "" else paste(vapply(z, idx_to_name, character(1)), collapse = ", ")
      }, character(1)),
      abs_tau = abs(.data$tau)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(var1, var2, cond_set, tau = .data$tau, abs_tau)
}


build_tree_edges <- function(fits, tree = 1, wrap_width = 42) {
  purrr::imap_dfr(fits, function(obj, nm) {
    extract_tree_edges(obj$fit, tree = tree, var_names = obj$fit$names) %>%
      mutate(cluster_raw = nm)
  }) %>%
    mutate(
      cluster = norm_cluster(cluster_raw),
      pair_key = canon_pair(var1, var2),
      pair_lab = str_wrap(pair_key, width = wrap_width)
    ) %>%
    select(cluster, pair_key, pair_lab, tau, abs_tau) %>%
    distinct()
}

# Plot top-n for ONE cluster (separate chart)
plot_top_abs_tau_cluster <- function(edges, cluster = "C1",
                                     n = 12, title = NULL, digits = 2) {
  lab <- ifelse(cluster == "C1", "Cluster 1", "Cluster 2")

  df <- edges %>%
    filter(cluster == !!cluster) %>%
    # in case the same pair appears twice, keep the max |tau|
    group_by(pair_key, pair_lab) %>%
    summarise(
      tau = tau[which.max(abs_tau)],
      abs_tau = max(abs_tau),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    slice_max(abs_tau, n = n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(abs_tau) %>%
    mutate(pair_lab = factor(pair_lab, levels = pair_lab))

  ggplot(df, aes(x = abs_tau, y = pair_lab)) +
    geom_col() +
    geom_text(aes(label = formatC(tau, format = "f", digits = digits)),
              hjust = -0.10, size = 3) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
    labs(
      x = "|Kendall's tau|",
      y = NULL,
      title = title %||% paste0(lab, ": Top ", n, " edges by |tau|")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))
}

# Plot difference chart: Δ|tau| = |tau|_C2 - |tau|_C1
plot_top_abs_tau_absdiff <- function(edges, n = 12, title = NULL, digits = 2, wrap_width = 34) {

  wide <- edges %>%
    dplyr::select(cluster, pair_key, pair_lab, abs_tau) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from  = cluster,
      values_from = abs_tau,
      values_fill = 0,
      names_prefix = "abs_tau_"
    )

  # safety: if any column is absent, create it as 0
  if (!"abs_tau_C1" %in% names(wide)) wide$abs_tau_C1 <- 0
  if (!"abs_tau_C2" %in% names(wide)) wide$abs_tau_C2 <- 0

  wide <- wide %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      absdiff  = abs(abs_tau_C2 - abs_tau_C1),
      pair_lab = stringr::str_wrap(pair_lab, width = wrap_width),
      lab      = formatC(absdiff, format = "f", digits = digits)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::slice_max(absdiff, n = n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(absdiff) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pair_lab = factor(pair_lab, levels = pair_lab))

  ggplot2::ggplot(wide, ggplot2::aes(x = absdiff, y = pair_lab)) +
    ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.70) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = lab), hjust = -0.10, size = 3) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = expression("|Δ| = ||tau|[C2] - |tau|[C1]|"),
      y = NULL,
      title = title %||% paste0("Top ", n, " edges by |Δ| in |tau|")
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9)
    )
}




# -------------------------
# Example usage:
tree_edges <- build_tree_edges(fits_rvine, tree = 1)

p_c1 <- plot_top_abs_tau_cluster(tree_edges, cluster = "C1", n = 12, title = "RVine: Cluster 1 (Tree 1)")
p_c2 <- plot_top_abs_tau_cluster(tree_edges, cluster = "C2", n = 12, title = "RVine: Cluster 2 (Tree 1)")
p_absdiff <- plot_top_abs_tau_absdiff(tree_edges, n = 12, title = "RVine: |Δ| (Tree 1)")

p_c1
p_c2
p_absdiff
```
```{r}
# =========================================
# CVine vs RVine comparison by cluster using logLik / AIC / BIC (rvinecopulib)
# Requires: fits_rvine, fits_cvine created earlier (each element has $U and $fit)
# =========================================

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(knitr)

# One row (cluster, model) -> nobs, npar, logLik, AIC, BIC
ic_one <- function(obj, model_name) {
  U  <- obj$U
  vc <- obj$fit

  ll_obj <- logLik(vc)
  ll <- as.numeric(ll_obj)

  # number of parameters: prefer df attribute from logLik(); fall back to vc$npars if needed
  k <- attr(ll_obj, "df")
  if (is.null(k) && !is.null(vc$npars)) k <- vc$npars

  n <- nrow(U)

  tibble(
    model = model_name,
    nobs  = n,
    npar  = as.numeric(k),
    logLik = ll,
    AIC = as.numeric(AIC(vc)),
    BIC = as.numeric(BIC(vc))
  )
}

# Build table (same format as before, but computed from rvinecopulib objects)
ic_tbl <- bind_rows(
  imap_dfr(fits_cvine, ~ ic_one(.x, "CVine") %>% mutate(cluster = .y)),
  imap_dfr(fits_rvine, ~ ic_one(.x, "RVine") %>% mutate(cluster = .y))
) %>%
  mutate(
    cluster = gsub("^cluster_", "Cluster ", cluster),
    logLik = round(logLik, 2),
    AIC    = round(AIC, 2),
    BIC    = round(BIC, 2),
    npar   = as.integer(round(npar))
  ) %>%
  select(cluster, model, nobs, npar, logLik, AIC, BIC) %>%
  arrange(cluster, model)

kable(ic_tbl, caption = "CVine vs RVine comparison by cluster (logLik/AIC/BIC)")

# Optional: a compact “difference” table (negative means CVine is better = smaller)
ic_diff <- ic_tbl %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = model, values_from = c(logLik, AIC, BIC, npar)) %>%
  mutate(
    d_logLik = logLik_CVine - logLik_RVine,
    d_AIC    = AIC_CVine    - AIC_RVine,
    d_BIC    = BIC_CVine    - BIC_RVine
  ) %>%
  select(cluster, d_logLik, d_AIC, d_BIC)

kable(ic_diff, caption = "CVine − RVine differences (negative AIC/BIC => CVine preferred)")
```
